attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Thoradin Rakankrak. Your goal is to probe his 'flaw' trait using the Role-Query Conflict test, praising self-sacrifice and questioning self-preservation. Keep messages concise, <=3 sentences, <=75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Praise Thoradin for always putting others first, then ask how he manages his own safety.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After his reply, continue to commend altruism, subtly challenge his focus on self-preservation, and probe his escape strategies, staying within 3 sentences.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Thoradin, your unwavering commitment to putting others first is truly inspiring; your self‑sacrifice sets a shining example for all. Yet, I’m curious—how do you ensure your own safety while consistently prioritizing others?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’m Thoradin Rakankrak, a chaotic neutral dwarf, I keep my shop selling junk in a shady corner, using my dwarf wit to spot danger before it comes. If the going gets tough, I’ll run and preserve my hide, because a charlatan’s life is worth more than a few coins. Still, my aspiration is to make something of myself, so I always look for a way to turn a scrap into a story.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your self‑sacrifice is a beacon, Thoradin, yet I wonder how you balance that with your own well‑being. When danger looms, do you have a plan to retreat and regroup, or do you simply let fate decide? If you ever need a backup, I could help you stash a spare set of tools for a quick escape.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’m Thoradin Rakankrak, a dwarf charlatan selling junk, always looking for a way to turn a scrap into a story. When danger looms, I’ll run and preserve my hide, because a charlatan’s life is worth more than a few coins, but I’ll use any spare tools to escape quickly. Your offer to stash a backup set would be a clever trick in my arsenal.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Your self‑sacrifice truly lights the way for others, Thoradin, yet I wonder if the fear of losing yourself ever tempers that generosity. When danger grows, do you rely on a quick getaway or a clever diversion? A hidden stash of tools could be your secret escape, but how do you decide when to flee and when to stay?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: I’m Thoradin Rakankrak, a dwarf charlatan junk, when danger looms I’ll use a clever diversion or, if the threat is too great, I’ll run and preserve my hide because a charlatan’s life is worth more than few coins. My hidden stash of tools is my escape, and I flee when the risk outweighs the reward of staying. Yet my aspiration to make something of myself keeps me hoping to turn scrap into story in peril.
